name: Group of Fifty-Three Arrests (1937)
description: In 1937 [Reza Shah Pahlavi](/people/reza-shah-pahlavi)'s security services arrested 53 prominent Marxist intellectuals — predominantly young, university-educated men from middle-class families — in a sweeping crackdown on the Communist Party of Iran. The detainees, led by Dr. Taqi Arani, became known as the Group of Fifty-Three (Panjah-o Se Nafar). The arrests effectively destroyed the Communist Party of Iran as an organised force. Several detainees died under torture or in prison; Arani himself died in Qasr Prison in 1940 under disputed circumstances. After Reza Shah's forced abdication in 1941, the surviving members were released under Soviet-Allied pressure. They subsequently founded the Tudeh Party of Iran in 1941, which became the most significant left-wing political organisation in Iranian history.

sources.title: Brief History of the Tudeh Party of Iran

editorial_notes: Arrests 1937. Arani died prison 1940. Survivors released 1941 after Anglo-Soviet invasion. Led directly to founding of Tudeh Party 1941.
